title: Historical Interactive LarePass Profile Rehydration And Mesh Validation
historical_note: This packet records one bounded Olares Phase 5 interactive profile-rehydration slice from before the canonical repo boundary moved to `C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform` on 2026-05-07. It remains packet-history provenance, not a live execution instruction for current repo operations.
current_routing: use PROJECT_STATUS.md for the current residue-retirement lane and latest completed packets
use docs/architecture/OLARES-PUBLICATION-BOUNDARY-RETIREMENT-DEPENDENCY-INVENTORY-2026-05-06.md for the remaining post-cutover boundary closeout queue
use this packet JSON only when historical provenance is needed for the earlier `olares-phase-5-004` interactive profile-rehydration slice
objective: Use an operator-mediated LarePass or Olares UI login or start path to recreate the missing local profile state, then validate whether the workstation receives a real node key, a `100.64.*` mesh IP, peer `olares`, and working SSH to `100.64.0.1`, while capturing the tightened config evidence required by Packet 003.
dependency_note: Packet 003 closed as research-only and concluded that the likely blocker is local LarePass or TermiPass profile state rather than basic Headscale endpoint reachability. It recommended an interactive local profile rehydration path as the least invasive next packet and tightened the exact config evidence that must be captured.
required_inputs: The Packet 003 ruling that local profile state is the likely blocker and that LarePass VPN over Tailscale or Headscale is the intended private path, recorded in ops/agents/handoffs/2026-05-03-olares-phase-5-003-termpass-needslogin-blocker-audit-and-recovery-path-research-handoff.md

The 2026-05-01 successful recovery recipe and proof surfaces in ops/agents/handoffs/2026-05-01-olares-private-stack-browser-terminal-bring-up-handoff.md and docs/architecture/OLARES-SSH-HOSTKEY-RECONCILIATION-2026-05-01.md
Current workstation local access to the LarePass or Olares desktop UI plus the TermiPass named-pipe local API
Operator availability to complete interactive desktop login or MFA if required
Host-side Headscale registration path only if a real non-zero pending node key appears during this packet
constrained_outputs: A dated handoff recording whether interactive local profile rehydration created a real profile, non-zero node key, mesh IP, peer visibility, and working SSH to `100.64.0.1`

Captured config evidence for local LarePass profile and VPN status, local TermiPass `status`, `prefs`, and `profiles`, Headscale device list or pending-node visibility for this workstation, SSH-over-VPN setting status, and private route plus SSH reachability to `100.64.0.1`
A statement of whether VS Code Remote-SSH is newly viable or remains blocked
A statement of whether the runtime-inventory portion of Packet 001 can now be re-opened under a separate or follow-on packet

No installs, no promotions, no ingress changes, no auth model changes, no AI-toolchain rollout, no hosting changes, and no host-runtime mutation beyond tightly bounded node registration if a real pending node exists and that exact step is required
validation_steps: Open LarePass or the relevant Olares desktop profile app interactively and confirm current account or session state through the UI.
Use the LarePass VPN or network-management UI to start or enable the private mesh without widening scope into generic client reinstall or unbounded settings changes.
Capture local LarePass profile and VPN status as explicit evidence rather than inferring it from the TermiPass adapter alone.
Capture local TermiPass named-pipe `status`, `prefs`, `profiles/current`, and `profiles/` after the interactive recovery attempt.
Capture whether the workstation appears in Headscale device status or whether a real pending node key is now visible.
Capture whether SSH-over-VPN is allowed or applied if the LarePass or Olares UI exposes that state.
Validate adapter IP state, the `100.64.*` route table, `Test-NetConnection 100.64.0.1 -Port 22`, and non-interactive SSH using a temporary `UserKnownHostsFile`.
Perform the already-documented Headscale registration step only if a real non-zero pending node key appears and only if the packet execution context explicitly permits that bounded action.
If profile rehydration does not create a real profile or node identity, record the exact blocker and stop without widening into service restarts, installs, or client-store reset work.
sql_permission_note: This packet authorizes interactive local access recovery and bounded connectivity validation only. It does not authorize database writes, service installs, broad client repair, or host-runtime mutation beyond the specifically bounded node-registration step if a real pending node appears.
closure_note: Not yet executed. This packet exists because Packet 003 concluded that the likely blocker is local LarePass or TermiPass profile state and recommended interactive local profile rehydration as the least invasive next bounded method.
next_action: Execute this packet if operator interaction is available. If interactive local login or VPN-state recovery is not available, prefer the explicit browser-terminal host-inventory fallback packet instead of retrying Packet 002 unchanged.
